meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no marketing skills (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no marketing skills. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: Overcome Marketing Gaps
content.seo_description: Stop guessing your revenue. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to implement high-converting SaaS pricing models and bypass common marketing skill gaps.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing degree to win at pricing. Use value-based tiering to let the product sell itself. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural structure and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can automate your pricing logic, ensure high conversion rates, and deliver professional, high-value SaaS assets to your clients instantly.
content.intro:
The cold reality of freelancing is that your code is only as valuable as your ability to monetize it. You've spent weeks perfecting the architecture, but when it comes time to set the pricing, the silence is deafening. You aren't a marketer, and the fear of 'leaving money on the table' or 'pricing yourself out of the market' is paralyzing your progress.

But here is the secret: pricing isn't about marketing fluff; it's about data-driven psychology. By treating your pricing model as a technical feature rather than an afterthought, you remove the guesswork. We are going to build a high-conversion pricing engine that does the heavy lifting for you, allowing you to ship professional-grade SaaS products that practically sell themselves.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Establish three distinct tiers based on user outcome rather than feature lists. This removes the need for complex marketing copy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing schema with three tiers: 'Starter' for individual creators, 'Pro' for scaling teams, and 'Enterprise' for custom scale. Include JSON structure for features and pricing constants.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing_config.json file based on the Antigravity schema provided.'

steps.title: Automate Pricing Logic
steps.description: Implement the backend logic that calculates annual vs. monthly discounts automatically to increase lifetime value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript utility function that calculates annual savings and returns the final price object for the frontend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the discount calculator function into src/utils/pricing.ts and add unit tests.'

steps.title: Build the UI Component
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing table component that highlights the 'Pro' tier as the recommended option.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Tailwind CSS pricing table component with a 'Most Popular' badge on the middle card. Ensure it is fully responsive.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the PricingTable.tsx component and integrate it into the landing page.'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout
steps.description: Map your pricing tiers to Stripe Price IDs to ensure seamless payment processing without custom billing code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe integration service that maps our local tier IDs to Stripe API price objects for checkout sessions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Set up the Stripe checkout handler in the API routes using the provided mapping.'

steps.title: Validate Conversion Loops
steps.description: Set up automated logging to track which tier is clicked most, providing data for future pricing adjustments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add an event tracking hook to the pricing table that sends click data to our analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate analytics tracking into all pricing button clicks and verify with a test event.'

pro_tips: Always anchor your 'Pro' tier price by placing a significantly more expensive 'Enterprise' tier next to it.
Use yearly billing as the default toggle; it increases upfront cash flow and reduces churn by 30%.
Never list features as 'Basic/Advanced'; list them as 'Solves X/Solves Y' to align with user pain points.
Include a 'Contact Sales' button for Enterprise instead of a price to capture high-intent leads.
Implement 'Social Proof' badges like 'Used by 500+ Freelancers' directly inside the pricing card to build instant trust.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing engine to a staging environment, run a load test on the checkout flow, and start A/B testing your tier names to see which resonates best with your specific niche.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your lack of marketing expertise block your revenue potential. Use these tools to engineer your success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
